name: UNLV doctor explains breakthrough as experimental pill gives hope for cancer treatment
summary: Research shows that a novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer. The drug is called daraxonrasib and it blocks a mutated protein that fuels tumor growth in more than 90% of pancreatic cancer cases — a target that had eluded treatment for decades.

name: The Alarming Ice Cream Shop Red Flag We Just Can't Ignore
summary: The nostalgia of old-fashioned ice cream parlors is hard to resist, whether genuine throwbacks to years gone by or modern versions inspired by the past. Spots filled with huge tubs and metal scoops of fresh ice cream, offering a parade of different flavors and toppings. There's also the aromas of subtly sweet caramelized sugar, fresh dairy, vanilla, and the toasty warmth of house-made waffle cones. What if those well-loved scents were missing, replaced by something decidedly unpleasant?

name: Coping With Colorectal Cancer
summary: Statistically speaking, the 5-year relative survival rate for Stage IIA colorectal cancer is generally high, estimated at over 90%.

name: UNLV mosquito lab: No local mosquitoes have tested positive for West Nile so far
summary: Louisa Messenger, a researcher at UNLV’s mosquito lab, said less than 1 percent of people with West Nile virus develop neuroinvasive cases. The virus crosses the blood-brain barrier and causes symptoms like meningitis and inflammation of the tissues of the brain.

name: Trump’s Vegas Enforcer Accused of Scaring Off Her Staff
summary: A Trump-appointed prosecutor has been accused of using her role to benefit her personal interests and creating a staff exodus due to her behavior.

name: Pending casino deals and turn to machine games are raising Las Vegas Strip job questions
summary: Billionaire buyers circling two of the biggest casino companies on the Las Vegas Strip are raising fresh questions about what comes next for tens of thousands of hospitality jobs in Southern Nevada, especially as more properties experiment with electronic gaming and automation.

name: UNLV expert outlines West Nile virus symptoms and bite-prevention steps for residents
summary: Southern Nevada is seeing its first reported case of West Nile virus this season, with Clark County confirming an infection in a local resident.

name: Common medications may disrupt brain development
summary: Many people don't think twice when their doctor prescribes antidepressants or other medications, but new research suggests that some of these drugs may subtly interfere with brain development in unborn babies, raising urgent questions about their safety during pregnancy.

name: Daylight saving time linked to heightened mental health risks, review finds
summary: Study comes amid growing calls to end the seasonal practice of changing clocks in the U.S.
